question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `accountBalanceFileExists` AtomicBoolean to be permanently latched to `true` by injecting a crafted balance file that passes signature verification, so that all subsequent `shouldDownload()` calls return `false` when `downloaderProperties.isEnabled()` is `false`, permanently suppressing balance file ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `onVerified()` calls `accountBalanceFileExists.set(true)` as a one-way latch with no reset path, so that once any single balance file is verified the in-memory flag can never be cleared without a JVM restart, causing a permanent denial-of-service for balance downloads when the downloader is later disabled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` to throw an unchecked exception (e.g., via a database connection disruption they influence), which propagates out of `shouldDownload()` and crashes the scheduled `download()` invocation, resulting in a denial-of-service for balance file ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a malicious `AccountBalanceFile` whose `super.onVerified()` call records a negative `consensusStart` delta to the `streamCloseMetric`, corrupting monitoring data and masking real latency anomalies?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `downloadNextBatch()` (called from `download()`) to silently swallow a `SignatureVerificationException` via the `log.warn` path, allowing a forged balance file to be processed without alerting operators?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `shouldDownload()` returns `true` when both `downloaderProperties.isEnabled()` is `false` AND `accountBalanceFileExists` is `false` AND `findLatest()` returns empty, to force the downloader to fetch one balance file batch even when the operator intended to disable it, potentially ingesting attacker-controlled data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a balance file with an extremely large `consensusEnd` timestamp so that `cloudStorageLatencyMetric.record(Duration.between(consensusEnd, cloudStorageTime))` produces a negative or overflow duration, corrupting Micrometer metrics and misleading operators about system health?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the cloud storage bucket with a large number of crafted `.csv_sig` signature files so that `downloadAndParseSigFiles()` (invoked via `download()`) downloads and parses an unbounded number of signatures, exhausting heap memory and causing an OOM crash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of checks in `shouldDownload()` — where `downloaderProperties.isEnabled()` is checked before `accountBalanceFileExists` — to force a redundant database query to `findLatest()` on every scheduled tick when the downloader is disabled and no file has been verified yet, causing excessive database load?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `super.onVerified(streamFileData, streamFile)` to throw a `RuntimeException` after `accountBalanceFileExists.set(true)` has already been called, leaving the flag permanently set to `true` even though the file was not fully processed, causing future balance downloads to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `download()` has no rate-limiting or back-pressure beyond the `fixedDelayString` scheduler, so that if the scheduler thread pool is saturated by other tasks, multiple `download()` invocations queue up and execute concurrently, causing duplicate balance file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `account_balance_file` database table (e.g., via SQL injection in an upstream component) to make `findLatest()` return a stale or attacker-controlled record, causing `accountBalanceFileExists` to be set to `true` and suppressing legitimate balance file downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file where `streamFile.copy().clear()` in `super.onVerified()` retains a reference to large byte arrays, causing a memory leak that grows with each verified file and eventually leads to an OOM condition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `downloadNextBatch()` to permanently disable the downloader by placing a balance file with a timestamp after `importerProperties.getEndDate()` in the cloud bucket, triggering `downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false)` and preventing all future balance ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic sequence of `accountBalanceFileExists.get()` followed by `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` in `shouldDownload()` to cause a double-download of the same balance file batch under concurrent scheduler invocations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a balance file with a `consensusStart` value of `Long.MIN_VALUE` so that the `streamCloseMetric.record(latency, TimeUnit.NANOSECONDS)` call in `super.onVerified()` records a negative latency, causing metric histogram corruption?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEMO network short-circuit in `downloadNextBatch()` — where `downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false)` is called when `sigFilesMap.isEmpty()` — to permanently disable balance downloading on a DEMO node by ensuring the bucket appears empty during the first poll?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()` to return a non-empty `Optional` containing a corrupted `AccountBalanceFile` object, causing `accountBalanceFileExists.set(true)` to be called with invalid state and suppressing all future downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `StreamFileData` object with a `lastModified` timestamp far in the future, causing `cloudStorageLatencyMetric.record()` to record an astronomically large duration that overflows the metric bucket and corrupts monitoring dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `download()` catches all `Exception` types in `downloadNextBatch()` and only logs them, so that a repeated exception (e.g., from a malformed balance file) silently fills log storage and causes log-based alerting to miss the attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `shouldDownload()` has no synchronization around the compound check-then-act sequence (`accountBalanceFileExists.get()` → `findLatest()` → `accountBalanceFileExists.set(true)`), allowing two concurrent scheduler threads to both pass the check and both call `downloadNextBatch()` simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a balance file that causes `streamFileNotifier.verified(streamFile)` (called in `super.onVerified()`) to throw an unchecked exception, which propagates before `accountBalanceFileExists.set(true)` is called in the subclass, leaving the flag `false` and causing the downloader to re-download the same file on the next tick?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user place a `.pb.gz` balance file with a decompression bomb payload in the cloud bucket, causing `StreamFileData.decompressBytes()` (invoked during `streamFileReader.read()` in `verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()`) to exhaust heap memory and crash the importer?
